article_meta.title: Why a nation of 1.45 billion wants more children16 December 2024ShareSaveSoutik BiswasIndia correspondentShareSaveAFPLas

reasoning: Mixed performance: two genuine author-prose bias instances correctly flagged, but one clear attribution violation (quoted, attributed statement flagged as author bias) and at least one notable missed instance in the author’s own framing.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged an attributed quote (“In other words, Mr Goli says, ‘India is getting old before getting rich’”) as author bias, violating the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: One of three items was a clear false positive due to being quoted/attributed; the other two were valid bias instances.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear author-level framing such as “As if the southern states’ concerns weren’t enough,” and vague sourcing like “many fear,” which could indicate Omission of Source Attribution.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: For the two valid items, labels were appropriate (subjective adjectives and sensational/conversational tone). The mislabeling concern applies to the invalid, quoted item.
